regime: Trend Flip Down / Risk-Off Reversal (Volatility Re-Expanding)
summary: The post-FOMC, holiday-era uptrend broke today. On the first full session after Juneteenth, equities gapped to fresh highs overnight on a weekend US-Iran peace roadmap, then reversed hard at the cash open as the AI/semiconductor/momentum unwind reasserted. MNQ and MES both flipped TSMOM-20 SHORT at the 14:00 UTC bar (MNQ closed 30,662 below the new 30,863 stop; MES 7,540.75 below 7,582). Crude round-tripped a weekend supply-scare spike (78.50 to a 73.50 low) and flipped short on the 60-day Iranian-oil authorization, closing 74.05. Gold was the lone trend that held, firming its long to 4,209.8 above a rising 4,179 stop, a clean haven bid. ATR(14) re-expanded; volatility is back. TSMOM-20 (long-only) is now FLAT, stopped out of its long, and stands aside until a confirmed reclaim above 30,863.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 long re-arm watch (Tue)
setups.entry: No long while price is below the flipped 30,863 stop. Re-arm only on a confirmed hourly close back above 30,863
setups.bias: The long was stopped out at the flip. Long-only means flat is the position; do not buy under the stop. Wait for the reclaim.

setups.name: Gold haven long (continuation)
setups.entry: Holds above the 4,179 rising stop on hourly closes; dips toward 4,179 are the trend-support zone
setups.bias: The only intact trend, now confirmed rather than fragile. Size with discipline into risk-off; losing 4,179 ends it.

setups.name: Short-side context (educational, not a TSMOM signal)
setups.entry: Discretionary only: failed retests of 30,863 / 30,621 AVWAP that reject, or acceptance below 30,503
setups.bias: TSMOM-20 is long-only and takes NO shorts. This is regime context for discretionary traders, not a system signal. Respect the flip; do not fade it by forcing longs.

event.name: No scheduled US release (weekend US-Iran de-escalation drove the tape)
event.consensus: After Friday's quiet Juneteenth half-session, the question was whether the post-FOMC repair would hold on full participation.
event.actual: It broke. A weekend US-Iran roadmap toward a 60-day peace deal, a US Treasury authorization of Iranian oil exports, and Gulf supply restarts collapsed crude's risk premium. Equities gapped to fresh highs then reversed; NQ and ES flipped short; crude flipped short; gold caught a haven bid.
event.surprise: Risk-off reversal. The gap to new highs was sold from the open, an outside-down distribution day.
event.nqReaction: MNQ gapped to a 30,967.75 high then reversed, flipping TSMOM SHORT at 14:00 UTC; closed 30,662 below the new 30,863 stop. ATR(14) re-expanded to 117.1. TSMOM-20 long stopped out, now flat.
event.crossAsset: MES flipped short with NQ (7,540.75 below 7,582). MCL gapped to 78.50 then crashed ~6% to a 73.50 low, flipping short below 75.18 (close 74.05). MGC held long and firmed to 4,209.8 above a rising 4,179, a haven rotation.

observe.name: GAP_NQ
observe.note: The overnight gap to fresh highs fully reversed, a textbook gap-up fade that paid. Clean fade environment, but the strategy is monitoring only (not deployed).

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ
observe.note: The short-horizon trend flipped short today; watch whether the long-horizon 252 filter also rolls over or holds the broader uptrend. No deployment.

risk.throttleState: DEFENSIVE / REDUCED. Trend flipped and volatility re-expanded. The long-only stack is flat.
risk.ddProximity: Elevated-but-contained. TSMOM-20 took the trailing-stop exit on the flip; the stop capped the give-back from the highs.
risk.dailyLossBudget: The flip exit consumed the day's trend-following risk; no new adds warranted.
risk.sizing: NQ and ES are BELOW their ATR stops, so the long habitat is OFF and long-only takes no shorts: flat. Gold is the only intact trend (confirmed long above 4,179). Crude long flipped to flat.
risk.note: Volatility re-expanded and the equity trend flipped, so the binding constraint is whipsaw risk around the freshly flipped stops. Stay DEFENSIVE until either a confirmed reclaim re-arms the long or the downtrend builds structure. Week ahead: regional Fed surveys Tuesday; final Q1 GDP and jobless claims Thursday Jun 25; May PCE inflation Friday Jun 26 is the catalyst.

psychology: The hardest moment today was the gap to new highs that pulled longs in minutes before it reversed. The lesson is structural, not emotional: a trailing stop flipping is information, not an inconvenience. Respect the flip, take the flat, and do not revenge-long beneath a broken stop. For a long-only system, flat is a position, and protecting capital through a reversal is the win. Let Tuesday show whether this is a one-day shakeout or the start of a real downtrend before re-engaging.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidence
DirectionDOWNTREND FLIP (equities). MNQ flipped TSMOM SHORT at 14:00 UTC (30,662 below the new 30,863 stop; prior long 30,460.67). MES confirmed (7,541 below 7,582; prior 7,525.58). Crude flipped SHORT (74.05 below 75.18; prior long 74.74). Gold HELD LONG and firmed (4,210 above a rising 4,179).High
VolatilityRE-EXPANDING. ATR(14) jumped (MNQ 87.7 to 117.1; MES 15.3 to 18.8). Wide ranges returned (MNQ RTH 465 pts, overnight 601 pts; crude overnight 5.00). The dormant regime is over.High
SessionTREND-DOWN REVERSAL / outside-down. Gap to fresh highs rejected, sold from the cash open, closed in the lower third of the RTH range. Distribution, not accumulation.High
InventorySHORT / heavy. Overnight longs that chased new highs were trapped; sellers controlled from the open. Net short lean into the close.Med-High
EventPOST_EVENT (geopolitical de-escalation) + PRE-PCE. Weekend US-Iran roadmap collapsed the oil risk premium. No first-tier US release Monday; May PCE Friday Jun 26 is the week catalyst.High
LiquidityNORMAL / full participation. First full session post-Juneteenth; real two-way overnight then one-way RTH distribution.High

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 19 map, checked on the Jun 22 session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultGrade
1MNQ holds above 30,461 rising ATR stop on hourly closes (uptrend intact)TRIGGEREDFAILED. RTH closes held above 30,461 (close 30,662), but the live stop ratcheted to 30,863 and flipped short at the 14:00 UTC open. The level held; the uptrend did not. TSMOM-20 long stopped out.
2MNQ loses 30,461 on an hourly close (repair stalls)NOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. No RTH hourly close lost 30,461 (lowest 30,556). The lone sub-level print was a thin Sunday-globex close (30,354); by session standard, not triggered.
3MES holds 7,526 stop / 7,500 psych (risk-on intact, aligned long)TRIGGEREDFAILED. MES held the old 7,526 and 7,500 (RTH low 7,527), but its stop ratcheted to 7,582 and flipped short with NQ. Aligned, but down not up.
4MGC holds above its 4,146 flipped stop on hourly closes (fragile long persists)TRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. Held every hourly close (stop rose 4,146 to 4,179), closed 4,209.8 (+0.9%). The fragile long firmed into a confirmed haven long.
5MCL holds 74.74 rising stop / 75 psych (crude long intact)TRIGGEREDFAILED. Crude lost both 75 and 74.74, flipping short (close 74.05, low 73.22) on the US-Iran de-escalation. The long failed.

Map for Tuesday, June 23, 2026. Filters: not Mon/Wed, no FOMC/CPI, so BIR, HOF, MID, BNC all eligible (HOF 12:00-14:00 ET dead zone). Calendar: no first-tier US release Tuesday (regional Fed surveys only); week catalyst May PCE Friday Jun 26, final Q1 GDP and jobless claims Thursday Jun 25. All levels from today's 1hr chart.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: The trend held in name only: NQ, ES and crude all held their stated old levels into the close yet their live ATR stops ratcheted up and flipped short, so rows 1, 3, 5 triggered but FAILED their bullish theses. Row 4 (gold) triggered and PLAYED OUT, firming into a confirmed long. Row 2 not triggered (no RTH close lost 30,461).
